title: Research methodology
summary: 9990 — experiments, observations, self-reports, and data analysis for Paper 2.
learningObjectives: Design experiments with IV, DV, controls, and conditions.
Evaluate observations, interviews, questionnaires, and case studies.
Calculate and interpret descriptive and inferential statistics.
Assess validity, reliability, ethics, and sampling.

simpleExplanation.title: How psychologists investigate
simpleExplanation.summary: Paper 2 tests whether you can design studies, spot flaws, and handle data — from hypotheses to chi-squared and beyond.
steps: Experimental method — lab, field, natural, quasi.
Data — mean, median, mode, SD, graphs.
Inferential stats — sign test, Wilcoxon, chi-squared, etc.

flashcards.back: Independent variable — manipulated by researcher; dependent variable — measured outcome.
Any variable other than the IV that could affect the DV — controlled to protect **internal validity**.
**Social desirability bias** — patients overreport adherence or underreport unhealthy behaviour.
Test of association between two **nominal/categorical** variables — e.g. treatment group vs recovery status.
Defining abstract variables in measurable terms — e.g. 'stress' measured by Perceived Stress Scale score.

flashcards.front: Ethical issue in health psychology experiments?
flashcards.back: **Protection from harm** — inducing stress or withholding treatment; requires debriefing, informed consent, and right to withdraw.

sections.content: **Research methodology** (3.1.2) is Paper 2. Practise **designing studies** and **interpreting stats** under timed conditions. Health Psychology topics (adherence, pain, stress) rely heavily on **self-report** and **field experiments** — always evaluate validity and ethics.

sections.question:
A researcher wants to test whether a text-message reminder system improves medication adherence in diabetic patients.

(a) Outline an experimental design for this study, identifying the IV, DV, and one control. [4 marks]
(b) Identify one ethical issue and one validity issue with this design. [4 marks]
sections.solution:
(a) **Experimental design:**
- **Design:** Independent groups — Group A receives **SMS reminders**; Group B receives **no reminders** (control).
- **IV:** Reminder condition (reminders vs no reminders).
- **DV:** **Adherence rate** — operationalised as pill count or biochemical marker (HbA1c as proxy for glucose control).
- **Control:** Match groups on age, diabetes type, and baseline adherence; standardise medication regimen.

(b) **Ethical issue:**
- **Confidentiality** — health data is sensitive; participants must consent to SMS contact and data storage under GDPR/medical ethics.

**Validity issue:**
- **Self-report adherence** (if used) lacks **validity** due to social desirability — patients may overreport compliance. Biochemical measures improve objectivity but increase cost.

**Additional evaluation:** Field experiment has **high ecological validity** (real-world setting) but less control over extraneous variables (e.g. family support) than a lab study.
